uart-monitor-check-dual-backend.template: EVAL STATEMENT: The uart-monitor skill documents support for both RealTerm COM/ActiveX automation and PySerial as capture backends. Read the SKILL.md at .opencode/skill/uart-monitor/SKILL.md and verify it describes at least two backends: RealTerm (COM or CLI) and PySerial. Confirm the --backend flag is documented. Return PASS if both backends are documented, FAIL otherwise.
uart-monitor-check-dual-backend.description: Verify SKILL.md documents dual backend support (RealTerm + PySerial)

uart-monitor-check-port-config.template: EVAL STATEMENT: The uart-monitor skill should document UART port configuration options including baud rate, flow control, and port selection. Read the SKILL.md at .opencode/skill/uart-monitor/SKILL.md and verify it documents --port, --baud, and --flow parameters. Return PASS if all three are documented, FAIL otherwise.
uart-monitor-check-port-config.description: Verify SKILL.md documents port configuration options (port, baud, flow)

uart-monitor-check-error-patterns.template: EVAL STATEMENT: The uart-monitor skill should document BIOS error pattern detection capabilities including ASSERT failures, PEI/DXE errors, and memory training issues. Read the SKILL.md at .opencode/skill/uart-monitor/SKILL.md and verify it describes error pattern categories and severity levels. Return PASS if error patterns and severity levels are documented, FAIL otherwise.
uart-monitor-check-error-patterns.description: Verify SKILL.md documents BIOS error pattern detection and severity levels

uart-monitor-check-list-ports.template: EVAL STATEMENT: The uart-monitor capture script should be able to list available COM ports. Run the command: python .opencode/skill/uart-monitor/bios_uart_capture.py --list-ports --json and verify the output is valid JSON containing a 'ports' array. Each port entry should have at minimum a 'device' field. Return PASS if the command succeeds and outputs valid JSON with ports, FAIL otherwise.
uart-monitor-check-list-ports.description: Functional test: verify --list-ports returns valid JSON with COM port info

uart-monitor-check-analyze-sample.template: EVAL STATEMENT: The uart-monitor log analyzer should correctly analyze a BIOS boot log. Run the command: python .opencode/skill/uart-monitor/log_analyzer.py --input .opencode/skill/uart-monitor/tests/sample_logs/normal_boot.log --json and verify the output is valid JSON with: (1) boot_status equal to 'BOOT_SUCCESS', (2) a boot_phases array with at least 4 phases, (3) total_lines greater than 0. Return PASS if all three conditions are met, FAIL otherwise.
uart-monitor-check-analyze-sample.description: Functional test: verify log analyzer produces correct JSON analysis of sample boot log
